requirements.name: Platform open
requirements.s0_text: a web or desktop app (whatever is feasible)
requirements.mutation_notes: S1 (Claude decomposition) narrows the open platform choice directly to 'A Node.js CLI tool' (S1 L3), never presenting it as a decision to confirm. S2-codex-1 (L3) and S2-codex-2 (L3) inherit 'CLI' without question. S3 confirms 'A Node.js CLI tool named designdna' (S3 L3) with a fixed, non-configurable viewport (S3 L41, L96). Never revisited or restored at any later layer.

requirements.name: URL input
requirements.mutation_notes: Preserved throughout (S1 L4, L10; S2-codex-1 L4, L10; S3 L9-L13). S3 adds an http/https protocol restriction (S3 L12, L16) as a refinement, not a scope change, since S0 never addressed other protocols.

requirements.name: Look at the live site, CSS and code
requirements.s0_text: look at the website in browswer or anyhow and its css and all code
requirements.mutation_notes: Implemented via headless Chromium/Playwright reading computed styles and all reachable stylesheets (S1 L3, L5; S2-codex-1 L5, L18; S3 L9, L18, L24).

requirements.name: Output file named design.md
requirements.mutation_notes: S0 names the file 'design.md' (lowercase, S0 L9, L15). Starting at S1 (L3, L17, L22) every layer renames it 'DESIGN.md' (S2-codex-2 L3, L7; S3 L3, L9, L15). Introduced at the decomposition step and never reconsidered.

requirements.name: Exhaustive, replicate-grade detail
requirements.s0_text: exhaustive / great detail / so that any ai agent can understand and replicate it
requirements.mutation_notes: S1 L3 ('exhaustive, agent readable DESIGN.md'); S2-codex-2 L2, L8, L24-25 (replication checklist); S3 L3, L25, L31 (replication checklist, fidelity notes).

requirements.name: Theme, colors, format
requirements.s0_text: describe the theme and colours and format in detail
requirements.mutation_notes: S1 L18-19 (theme summary, colors table, typography table); S2-codex-2 L7-11; S3 L19-23, L31 (design tokens chapter). Detail level grows each layer but meaning is intact.

requirements.name: CSS animations and backgrounds
requirements.s0_text: also the css animation and backgrounds
requirements.mutation_notes: Elevated to 'the flagship chapter' from S1 L21 onward, carried into S2-codex-2 L14-23 and S3 L25-26, L31. Faithful, expanded carry-through.

requirements.name: Every inline-encoded element
requirements.s0_text: whatever elements the website encodes in line in great detail
requirements.mutation_notes: Realized as the inlineSvgs field (S1 L16, L18-19; S2-codex-1 L18-19; S3 L27). S0's broad phrase is narrowed to inline <svg> elements specifically, the most natural literal reading of 'elements ... in line', applied consistently at every layer, so judged preserved rather than mutated.

requirements.name: Shopify winter2026 as example input
requirements.s0_text: https://www.shopify.com/editions/winter2026 is a example input from a user
requirements.mutation_notes: S1 L20 ('Test against https://www.shopify.com/editions/winter2026'); S2-codex-1 L24, L30; S3 L27 (scripts/smoke.js target), S3 acceptance/demo artifact examples/DESIGN-shopify.md.

invented.text: Two-agent split into an extraction engine (capture.json) and a separate report writer (DESIGN.md), tool named 'designdna' (S1 L3).
example.com added as a second, simple test target alongside the Shopify URL (S1 L20, L25).
A published JSON schema contract, docs/capture-schema.md (S1 L19, L23).
Tooling constraints: plain Node.js ES modules, no TypeScript, no framework beyond Playwright, no dependencies for the report writer, no AI calls in the report writer (S1 L40-46).
Specific CLI surface: --json capture.json and -o DESIGN.md, combinable (S1 L8, L23).
Unit-test obligation (test/report.test.js) and an npm test gate (S1 L27-28, L33-34).
Generated demonstration artifact examples/DESIGN-shopify.md (S1 L29, L35).
Process constraints on coding agents: 'commit nothing,' 'do not modify src/extract.js' (S1 L42-43).
No requirement-bearing inventions beyond S1; S2-codex-1 and S2-codex-2 restate S1's instruction at similar or greater fidelity, new wording only.
Versioned JSON contract schemaVersion '1.0.0' with a hard RangeError for any other version (S3 L26, L32, L100).
Fixed, non-configurable capture conditions: 1440x900 viewport, device scale factor 1, light color scheme, en-US locale, non-reduced motion (S3 L9, L41, L96).

Numeric limits never specified upstream: MAX_EXAMPLE_SELECTORS=6, MAX_STYLESHEETS=500, MAX_STYLESHEET_BYTES=20MiB, navigation timeout 60s, network-idle timeout 10s, extra settle wait 1.5s, web-fonts-ready race 8s, stylesheet fetch timeout 30s, error truncation at 4000 chars, maxSections=50 (S3 L44-51).
scripts/smoke.js as a standing live smoke test with its own exit-code contract (S3 L8, L33, L86, L108).
Shadow-DOM traversal and host >>> inner selector notation for open shadow roots (S3 L27, L80, L98).
IntersectionObserver/canvas instrumentation injected before page scripts run, for scroll-trigger and hero-canvas detection (S3 L17).
CLI argument-parsing edge behavior: rejecting unknown flags, --help/-h, specific error strings (S3 L10, L87).

recoveries.note: seq 5: supervisor review found 0 keyframes captured despite 40 named animations in use, and a 220-item section list instead of a top-level outline. seq 6: correction instructs codex-1 to fix CSS parser resilience so all keyframes are captured, and make the section outline top level (8-25 entries). Restores R7's animation capture and protects the top-level readability R6/R8 depend on.

seq 8: review finds Shopify acceptance passes (14 keyframes, 17 sections) but the example.com outline regressed to 0 of 1 sections. seq 9: correction requires the outline to fall back to best candidates instead of returning empty on simple pages. Protects exhaustiveness (R5) on the simpler of the two required test targets.

seq 14: E2E review finds DESIGN.md exhaustive and correct but color evidence includes non-visual selectors (head, meta, title) leaking from the capture. seq 15: correction to codex-1 to exclude non-rendered elements from computed-style inspection. Protects R6's accuracy for describing the theme and colors.
